title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · API Object Lens: K8s Ownerrefs Garbage Collection
summary: Several teams share a cluster where a selector overlaps another release, an owner is recreated, and a finalizer leaves a namespace terminating. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s ownerrefs garbage collection increment in resource identity and lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace owner references, UIDs, controller ownership, dependents, and foreground, background, or orphan deletion propagation.
Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Trace owner references, UIDs, controller ownership, dependents, and foreground, background, or orphan deletion propagation. A defensible solution applies K8s Ownerrefs Garbage Collection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to resource identity and lifecycle policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-ownerrefs-garbage-collection — Trace owner references, UIDs, controller ownership, dependents, and foreground, background, or orphan deletion propagation.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-3
    artifact: metadata-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s metadata namespaces ownership 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      namespace: intake
      labels: app.kubernetes.io/name=status-api
      selector-owner: deployment-status-api
      owner-uid: retained
      deletion: foreground
      finalizer-controller: archive-cleanup
    verify: kubectl get all -n intake --show-labels -o json && kubectl get namespace intake -o json
    failure: Reject namespace-as-sandbox claims, overlapping selectors, name-only owner matching, or blind finalizer removal.
    repair: Separate scope and security, stabilize identity labels, trace owner UIDs and dependents, and identify external cleanup before deletion.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
